paper_focus.title: Fatigue properties of Fe-30.5Mn-8Al-1C austenitic low-density steel: Critical impact of κ-carbide precipitation state
paper_focus.research_objective: To elucidate the effect of intragranular and intergranular κ-carbide precipitation states on the fatigue behavior and deformation mechanisms of Fe-30.5Mn-8Al-1C austenitic low-density steel, and to provide microstructural design strategies for improved fatigue resistance.
main_process_variables: Hot rolling to 4 mm thickness before heat treatment
Tensile test: room temperature, strain rate 10^{-3} s^{-1}
Fatigue test: stress-controlled tension-tension, R=0.1, frequency 20 Hz, sinusoidal waveform, ambient air
Specimen: flat dog-bone shape, loading axis parallel to rolling direction
